title: Regex Studio
description: A from-scratch regex workbench: parse to an AST, then take FOUR independent roads to one automaton — Thompson ε-NFA → subset construction, Brzozowski derivatives straight from the regex, Antimirov partial derivatives that build a tiny ε-free 'equation automaton' (collapsing (a|b|c)* to a single state where Thompson spends ten), and Glushkov's position automaton, ε-free with exactly one state per letter plus a start (the textbook (a|b)*abb → 6 states). All four minimise to the same canonical machine — itself reached two ways, by Moore and by Hopcroft's O(n log n) pass, cross-checked live. Run a string six ways — linear DFA, derivative DFA, streaming derivatives, the partial-derivative NFA, the position automaton, a linear-time Pike VM with captures, and a backtracking VM — and cross-check ten implementations (plus the platform's own RegExp) with a seeded differential fuzzer that already caught a real VM bug. Includes a static ReDoS analyser that proves catastrophic backtracking, synthesises the attack and prescribes the fix, equivalence checking with witnesses, language enumeration, DFA→regex synthesis, and DOT/SVG export.
